heading: Refusal of clearance and entry
label: 42305 Refusal of clearance and entry
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)4230546 App.:1710a(f)., , .Pub. L. 100–418, title X, § 10002(f)102 Stat. 1572Aug. 23, 1988Before paragraph (1), the words “Subject to ” are added to alert the reader to the application of that section. The word “determines” is substituted for “finds” for consistency with section 42306 of the revised title.section 42306 of this titleIn paragraph (1), the words “Secretary of Homeland Security” are substituted for “collector of customs at any port or place of destination in the United States” because the functions of the Customs Service and of the Secretary of the Treasury relating thereto were transferred to the Secretary of Homeland Security by section 403(1) of the Homeland Security Act of 2002 (, ). The functions of the collector of customs previously were vested in the Secretary of the Treasury by Reorganization Plan No. 26 of 1950, and the office of collector of customs previously was abolished by Reorganization Plan No. 1 of 1965.Pub. children.text: the Secretary of Homeland Security shall refuse the clearance required by to a vessel of a foreign carrier that is identified by the Commission under ; andsection 60105 of this titlesection 42304 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: deny entry, for purposes of oceanborne trade, of a vessel of a foreign carrier that is identified by the Commission under , to a port or place in the United States or the navigable waters of the United States; orsection 42304 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: detain the vessel at the port or place in the United States from which it is about to depart for another port or place in the United States.
